name: Georges Brassens
description:
'''Georges Charles Brassens''' ({{IPA|fr|ʒɔʁʒ(ə) ʃaʁl bʁasɛ̃s|lang}}; {{IPA|oc|ˈbɾasens|lang}}) was a French [[singer-songwriter]] and poet.

George Brassens is considered one of France's most accomplished postwar poets. He achieved fame through his elegant songs, characterised by harmonically complex music for voice and guitar, as well as articulate and diverse lyrics. He also [[musical setting|set to music]] poems by both relatively obscure poets and such well-known ones as [[Louis Aragon]] (''[[Il n'y a pas d'amour heureux]]''), [[Victor Hugo]] (''La Légende de la Nonne'', ''Gastibelza''), [[Paul Verlaine]], [[Jean Richepin]], [[François Villon]] (''La Ballade des Dames du Temps Jadis''), and [[Antoine Pol]] (''Les Passantes'').
